items.headline: Signs of life?: State of Crypto
items.subheadline: Several sources told CoinDesk that a new draft of the Clarity Act may drop this week, but challenges remain.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Europe led on crypto regulation. Now implementation must match ambition
items.author: Richard Teng

items.headline: Collateral, not yield, will decide which stablecoins win
items.subheadline: As yield-bearing stablecoins race toward a $50 billion market capitalization, the industry is optimizing for the wrong metric, argues Artem Tolkachev, chief RWA officer at Falcon Finance.
items.author: Artem Tolkachev

items.headline: Age verification is the surveillance nobody voted for
items.subheadline: KOSA and Chat Control both retreated on their most-criticized measures this week, but both kept mandatory age verification, the quiet mechanism that turns anonymous browsing into identified browsing for all of us, argues Billions CEO Evin McMullen.
items.author: Evin McMullen

items.headline: The UK has finally shown it’s serious about crypto
items.subheadline: Several recent regulatory steps indicate the UK might finally stop dragging its feet when it comes to crypto, argues Wirex CEO Chet Shah.
items.author: Chet Shah

items.headline: BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley join UK government's tokenization taskforce
items.subheadline: The 54 firm-strong group, which is backed by the City of London Corporation, will spend the next year working on live tokenisation use cases across UK financial markets
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: SBI Holdings' blockchain initiative pivots to Solana for tokenization, stablecoin issuance
items.subheadline: The SBI Solana Global joint venture now includes the Solana Foundation, the Zug, Switzerland-based organization that oversees the layer-1 network.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Strategy news: MSTR made no changes to BTC holdings last week as it raised cash
items.subheadline: The company raised the fresh cash via sales of common stock; its USD Reserve now stands at $3 billion.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Robinhood Chain scores strong debut, Bernstein says
items.subheadline: Robinhood's new blockchain quickly became a top-five network by DEX volume, signaling strong early demand for its tokenized asset platform, the broker said.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Luxembourg upgrades Ripple's preliminary crypto asset provider to fully compliant
items.subheadline: The approval means Ripple is now fully MiCA-compliant for payments, financial institutions, corporates and businesses across all 30 European Economic Area countries.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Stablecoins Were Meant to Disrupt Finance. Instead, They Became Idle Cash.
items.subheadline: O’Connor argues that crypto’s clearest success story has scaled as money but not as capital.
items.author: John O’Connor

items.headline: Vitalik Buterin calls lean Ethereum its biggest rebuild since the Merge
items.subheadline: A revised roadmap details how the multi-year overhaul would replace nearly every major part of Ethereum's protocol, with quantum resistance and privacy moving up the priority list. It lands as ether rallies more than 12% in the past seven days.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin slips below $63,000 in an Asian-session leverage flush
items.subheadline: The liquidations were minor, running at about a sixth of what the market saw at its worst over the past 30 days, per CoinGlass.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Over $7.2 billion have migrated from LayerZero to Chainlink CCIP as Mantle joins exodus
items.subheadline: Migrations to Chainlink CCIP so far include Kelp and Lombard, both of which brought over $1 billion, as well as Solv Protocol, Virtuals, Re, and Kraken’s tokenized assets.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Crypto lender giant Aave rolls out vaults for yield-hungry fintech investors
items.subheadline: Aave's new Stable Vaults product lets wallets, exchanges and payment apps offer yields on stablecoin deposits
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Grayscale's CFO exits after 7 years with crypto asset manager
items.subheadline: Edward McGee stepped down after seven years, months after distribution chief John Hoffman left for Ondo Finance.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Circle takes banking step with U.S. trust bank approval
items.subheadline: The company said the bank will initially serve Circle and its affiliates before potentially expanding services to financial institutions.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: OKX, MetaMask, Matter Labs back dispute resolution court for AI agents
items.subheadline: The Genlayer Foundation is leading the 27-firm consortium that makes AI-based payments, escrow and dispute resolution interoperable.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Hyundai adopts stablecoins for cross-border treasury transfers
items.subheadline: The project moved live funds between subsidiaries as businesses increasingly test blockchain for faster and lower-cost cross-border payments.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Crypto IPO pipeline slows amid weak market conditions
items.subheadline: Funding constraints and investor caution, not regulation, are delaying crypto IPOs, according to Cohen & Company Capital Markets' Christian Lopez.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: U.S. CPI, JPMorgan, Citi earnings reports: Crypto Week Ahead
items.subheadline: Your look at what's coming in the week starting July 13.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: AI chips and bitcoin show how powerful structural trends can still produce severe corrections
items.subheadline: Structural change can create lasting opportunities, but explosive rallies in semiconductors, metals and bitcoin show how quickly strong narratives can become speculative excess.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: U.S.-Iran hostilities over Strait of Hormuz drag crypto lower after positive week: Crypto Markets Today
items.subheadline: Crypto's weekend gains gave way to a Monday selloff as Middle East tensions resurfaced, South Korea's Kospi lost 9.2% and $253 million in leveraged positions were wiped out.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) developers embrace Vitalik Buterin's long-term vision but urge quicker execution
items.subheadline: The "Lean Ethereum" strawmap, updated earlier this week, has gained support from many of the network's researchers, though several say the biggest challenge won't be deciding what to build, but how quickly it can happen.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Live markets: Bitcoin and ether ETFs drew inflows Monday
items.subheadline: Bitcoin and ether ETFs drew fresh inflows Monday as the bid starts to return.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin price (BTC) falls back to $62,000 as Iran ceasefire collapses
items.subheadline: Hedge funds have turned the most bearish on the yen since 2007, boosting bets on further losses to nearly 138,000 contracts as of June 30.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ripple news: XRP Ledger v3.2.0 rollout gains ground but trails version its replacing
items.subheadline: The new software leads among the network's validators, yet by node count it sits behind the older v3.1.3, and the security amendment bundled with it remains a separate, slower vote.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: 'CASHCAT' trader turns $800 into over $1 million on Robinhood's brand new blockchain
items.subheadline: Robinhood's blockchain went live on July 1 to move stocks onchain. Its first breakout hit is a memecoin named after the mascot the company abandoned.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum's newest nonprofit wants to become Wall Street's guide to crypto
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we’re digging into Ethereum Institutional, a new nonprofit aimed at educating financial institutions and banks about Ethereum.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin takes aim at $65,000, erasing losses from earlier this week
items.subheadline: Spot bitcoin funds lost about $95 million on Thursday and ether funds roughly $52 million, ending the one bright spot in crypto's institutional flows even as prices rallied.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: ETH news: Ethereum Foundation says AI found bug that could take validators offline
items.subheadline: The Ethereum Foundation pointed coordinated AI agents at the software its validators run and got a remotely triggerable crash out of it. It also got a pile of confident, well-written findings that were not bugs at all.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: BTC news: Bitcoin’s BIP 110 fork deadline nears with miner support at zero
items.subheadline: The BIP 110 proposal would cap arbitrary data on Bitcoin for a year, but Michael Saylor, Adam Back and others say turning a spam dispute into a consensus fight could create a bigger risk than the spam itself.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: If America wants to lead in crypto, it must protect the people who build it
items.subheadline: Despite the Clarity Act’s advancement toward the finish line, there’s one provision under threat for builders that can’t be overlooked, argues Smith.
items.author: Kristin Smith

items.headline: Crypto’s security nightmare won’t be solved by ordinary audits
items.subheadline: Without an update to the current auditing infrastructure, the crypto space will likely continue to suffer significant losses, explains Beyer.
items.author: Stefan Beyer

items.headline: The SEC delayed tokenizing stocks, and here’s why that’s a relief
items.subheadline: Before we rush to tokenize U.S. equities, we need compliance systems that understand context, not just checkboxes. Investors like Michael Burry see the danger, so should the rest of us, urges Chamarajnagar.
items.author: Dr. Ravishankar Chamarajnagar

items.headline: The banking lobby is wrong about stablecoins and community banks
items.subheadline: Congress should not kneecap one of the clearest advances in payment infrastructure to protect community banks from a threat that has not been proven, argues Eco CEO Ryne Saxe.
items.author: Ryne Sax

items.headline: What Robinhood’s recent layoffs say about the current state of crypto investments
items.subheadline: Despite the trading platform’s restructuring and the wave of crypto companies making cuts, signaling a late bear market, Altcoin Pro’s Horst, Anderson and Zhuleku explain there’s no reason to panic.
items.author: Ryan Horst

items.headline: Tokenized securities need competition, not gatekeepers
items.subheadline: Innovation thrives when investors have choices. For tokenized securities, Washington shouldn't pick winners before the market has a chance to learn what works, argues Patrick McHenry, vice chairman at Ondo Finance and former Chairman of the House Financial Services Committee.
items.author: Patrick McHenry

items.headline: Europe is closing the door on offshore crypto, but it’s leaving the riskiest window open
items.subheadline: MiCA was never meant to address the giant crypto derivatives market. That could pose a serious problem, says Patrick Gruhn, founder and chief executive of Perpetuals.com.
items.author: Patrick Gruhn

items.headline: Bitcoin's U.S. reserve still a work-in-progress as federal agencies hash it out
items.subheadline: The White House says it's still evaluating the "best structure" for the federal fund to hold bitcoin as a long-term reserve and a separate stockpile of other crypto assets.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: U.S. SEC to propose crypto rule as soon as this month to ease startups, fundraising
items.subheadline: A newly updated agenda from the securities regulator shows so-called Reg Crypto high on its near-term list.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Reserve Bank of India (RBI) still favors crypto prohibition amid tax evasion fears
items.subheadline: Indian regulators maintain a hawkish stance despite growing embrace of digital assets and technology by governments worldwide.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Citadel abandons multi-year crypto lawsuit to focus on bankruptcy order against an ex-employee
items.subheadline: After winning a 6 million-pound London arbitration award, Citadel dropped its U.S. trade secrets case, saying another judgment would likely go uncollected.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Newest version of crypto Clarity Act may drop as soon as next week, sources say
items.subheadline: After a long stretch without breakthroughs in the crypto market structure bill, insiders say a new draft may emerge in a final push for late-July action — though it still lacks bipartisan buy-in.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: With SEC fight over, Coinbase's top legal exec Grewal moves on, and others reassigned
items.subheadline: Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal will depart the exchange, though he'll continue as an adviser, he said Thursday.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: New Hampshire snuffs out trailblazing bitcoin bond effort
items.subheadline: At its last stage for government approval, the state's executive council rejected the bond project 3-2.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: U.S. government digital dollar set to be banned tonight under housing law's CBDC limit
items.subheadline: Despite President Donald Trump's refusal to sign Congress' bipartisan housing bill, it's set to go into effect at midnight, and its temporary CBDC ban along with it.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Battle over blockchain stock ownership is heading to Washington regulators
items.subheadline: The Securities Transfer Association, an industry group for transfer agents, said company-authorized tokenization should receive preferential treatment under future rules.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Tom Lee's BitMine (BMNR) raises ether holdings to $5.77 million, or 4.8% of supply
items.subheadline: The company expanded its ETH treasury while Chairman Tom Lee pointed to Robinhood Chain's rapid growth on Ethereum.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Ripple news: XRP-linked firm lands inside UK plan for tokenized repo, bonds and funds
items.subheadline: A Treasury-backed report targets putting repo, gilts and funds onchain within two years, and warms to permissionless networks that firms like Ripple have been building on.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bolivia weighs adding Tether's USDT to its national payments system
items.subheadline: Crypto usage has spiked in the country, with transaction volumes hitting $430 million in the year after the central bank removed restrictions in mid-2024.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Robinhood's blockchain finds early success — Thanks to memecoins, not stocks
items.subheadline: Robinhood Chain has pulled in $135 million of value and 800,000 addresses since July 1. Almost none of it is doing what the chain was built for.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Strategy pauses bitcoin purchases as USD reserve reaches $3 billion
items.subheadline: Strategy now has more than 20 months of coverage for preferred-stock dividends and debt interest.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: The marginal bitcoin seller may be done liquidating, analysts say
items.subheadline: Analysts point to bitcoin’s resilience amid fresh U.S.-Iran escalation and renewed spot ETF inflows as the clearest signs yet that the marginal seller has finally stepped away. Some still remain cautious.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Circle's (CRCL) OCC approval fails to ease core concerns, Mizuho says
items.subheadline: Japanese investment bank Mizuho reiterated its neutral rating on Circle, saying OCC approval for a national trust bank doesn't address slowing USDC growth or rising competition.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Trump's crypto riches loom over Clarity Act talks to ban conflicts for U.S. officials
items.subheadline: Discussion among Democrats have fixed on the president's personal gains when hashing out the crypto market structure bill's ethics provision.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: TeraWulf CEO: 'Not All Megawatts Are Created Equally' in AI Race
items.subheadline: TeraWulf says its $19 billion AI hosting agreement with Anthropic underscores its transformation from a Bitcoin miner into an AI infrastructure company.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: Franklin Crypto CIO says crypto prices are disconnected from fundamentals
items.subheadline: Institutional adoption of crypto is accelerating even as digital asset prices fail to reflect the industry's strongest fundamentals in years, Seth Ginns said.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: Binance.US CEO says exchange is rebuilding, eyes return to 20% U.S. market share
items.subheadline: Binance.US is betting ultra-low fees, new regulated products and deeper liquidity will help it regain customers after two years of regulatory setbacks.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: BTC, XRP, ETH slip ahead of inflation report and Warsh testimony
items.subheadline: Major cryptocurrencies have dropped by 2% or more in 24 hours as traders boosted bets of a July Fed rate hike.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Solo BTC miner makes $200,000 using $150 equipment
items.subheadline: A solo miner just hit a full bitcoin block with a tiny $150 open-source rig.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: BTC, ETH news: U.S. government moves $288 million in seized bitcoin, ether to Coinbase Prime
items.subheadline: Coins from the Farace and BTC-e seizures moved through fresh wallets before landing on the exchange, despite president Donald Trump's previous no-sell reserve order.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin holds $62,600 as the Iran conflict reignites and CPI looms
items.subheadline: Trump reinstated the Hormuz blockade, sending oil higher and rate-hike bets up, reversing the peace trade that helped bitcoin recover in early July. Today's inflation print is the next test.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa
